message:
beads: file 459lp macOS/Windows sandbox parity gap (LOW)

Review surfaced Linux seccomp-bpf denies at the syscall boundary
while macOS sandbox_init denies at the Mach/BSD API layer and
Windows relies only on job-object process limits. Strict-profile
connectors still run under WASI so no host syscalls reach the
kernel, but native-profile parity is coarser on non-Linux. Logged
for follow-up; not urgent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.7","title":"[E2E/invoke] Verify W3C trace context propagates through entire invoke chain with structured logging","description":"The README says 'Every step is logged with W3C trace context (trace_id, span_id) for end-to-end distributed tracing.' Prove it: 1) Run an invoke through the full chain, 2) Collect structured log output from fwc, fcp-host, and the connector, 3) Verify a single trace_id is present in ALL components' log lines for this request, 4) Verify span_id hierarchy: fwc creates root span, fcp-host creates child span, connector creates grandchild span, 5) Verify the AuditEvent includes the trace_id and span_id, 6) Verify correlation IDs in JSON log output match across components. This is critical for production debugging - without trace propagation, diagnosing failures across the fwc->host->connector chain is impossible. TraceContext types are in fcp-audit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:36.852003Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:51:49.343043Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:51:49.342817Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:36.852003Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.7","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:37.626461Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2534,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.7","author":"jemanuel","text":"NOTE: correlation_id IS tracked and asserted in structured logs (host_connector_integration.rs:765). W3C trace_id/span_id (separate from correlation_id) is NOT yet implemented. This bead remains open for W3C trace context specifically, but the project is NOT without request correlation - it works via CorrelationId.","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:28:39Z"},{"id":2543,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.7","author":"jemanuel","text":"ASSESSMENT: Request correlation already works via CorrelationId (tested in integration tests, flows through invoke chain, present in structured logs). W3C Trace Context (trace_id + span_id format) is an interop enhancement, not a correctness gap. Downgraded to P2.","created_at":"2026-04-07T02:55:03Z"},{"id":2586,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.7","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: W3C trace context verified. Infrastructure already comprehensive: TraceContext types in fcp-core (binary) + fcp-telemetry (W3C format), to_traceparent/from_traceparent with full spec validation, inject/extract header helpers, 62 unit tests in fcp-telemetry. Added 4 cross-component contract tests: (1) invoke-to-audit trace_id propagation, (2) child span preserves trace_id with different span_id, (3) correlation_id independence from trace_context, (4) audit chain carries trace through linked events. 14 trace context tests pass.","created_at":"2026-04-07T18:51:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.8","title":"[E2E/invoke] Verify revocation enforcement: revoked capability token is rejected at runtime","description":"The README lists 'Revocation Enforcement' as a hard security invariant. Prove it: 1) Issue a valid capability token, invoke succeeds, 2) Create a RevocationObject for that token (owner-signed, with monotonic seq), 3) Publish the revocation to fcp-host, 4) Re-invoke with the same token - MUST be rejected with a revocation error, 5) Verify the rejection includes the revocation reason and seq, 6) Verify the revocation check is O(1) via monotonic seq comparison (not a list scan), 7) Also test: revoke an IssuerKey and verify all tokens minted by that key become invalid. The revocation code is in fcp-core with monotonic seq for O(1) freshness checks. This test proves security invariant #5 from the README.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:54.027193Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T02:54:37.124861Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T02:54:36.420263Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:54.027193Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:43:54.733664Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2542,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.8","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: Revocation enforcement is tested at multiple levels: 1) admin_state::revoke_token_success + verify_revoked_token_fails (6 unit tests pass), 2) enforcement.rs revocation_freshness check with 20+ tests (allow/deny/boundary/custom threshold), 3) fcp-core revocation.rs has 107 tests for RevocationRegistry::is_revoked(). The per-token revocation flow: is_token_revoked() checks ledger -> token inspection reports revoked status. Binary-level integration test is a nice-to-have but the logic is proven.","created_at":"2026-04-07T02:54:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.9","title":"[E2E/invoke] Verify rate limit enforcement: burst beyond limit is rejected with structured backoff","description":"The README says rate limiting is enforced via token bucket and sliding window with configurable burst. Prove it: 1) Configure a rate limit on the test connector's operation (e.g., 5 requests per 10 seconds), 2) Send 5 rapid invokes - all should succeed, 3) Send a 6th invoke immediately - MUST be rejected with a RATE_LIMITED error, 4) Verify the rejection includes retry_after information (how long to wait), 5) Wait for the window to reset, invoke again - should succeed, 6) Verify rate limiting is per-operation (different operations have independent budgets as declared in manifest.toml rate_limit pools). The rate limit code is in crates/fcp-ratelimit/. The manifest declares rate limits per operation in [provides.rate_limit]. This test proves the admission control claim from the README.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:49:00.134866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:28:12.103854Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T01:28:11.573368Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:49:00.134866Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T21:49:00.866092Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2533,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-423bu.3.9","author":"jemanuel","text":"CLOSED: Rate limiting has a dedicated 614-line integration test file (rate_limit_integration.rs) plus 24 mentions in the main integration suite. Budget enforcement verified.","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:28:12Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-459lp","title":"[LOW] macOS sandbox filters coarser than Linux seccomp","description":"Cross-platform sandbox parity gap surfaced during fcp-sandbox review.

Linux (crates/fcp-sandbox/src/linux.rs) uses seccomp-bpf to deny individual syscalls at the kernel boundary, with a built-per-policy allowlist including architecture validation (x86_64/aarch64) and an explicit SECCOMP_RET_KILL_PROCESS catch-all.

macOS (crates/fcp-sandbox/src/macos.rs:265-266, :459) relies on sandbox_init profiles which are coarser:
  - RLIMIT_NPROC is intentionally NOT set when deny_exec=true (NPTL threads count as procs, would starve the runtime).
  - Syscall-level denial is replaced by higher-level profile deny rules. Some denials that seccomp would KILL at the kernel the macOS profile only rejects at the Mach/BSD API layer.

Windows (crates/fcp-sandbox/src/windows.rs:134, :404) has no syscall filtering at all -- deny_exec relies on job-object process-limit. Even coarser than macOS.

Impact: a hostile connector exploiting a previously-unknown native-API path that sandbox_init does not cover may be blocked on Linux (seccomp deny-by-default) but reach kernel on macOS/Windows.

Not urgent: all three platforms still enforce the main deny_exec/deny_ptrace contract through some mechanism, and strict-profile connectors run under WASI (no host syscalls reach the kernel at all). Landing a tighter macOS profile or Windows integrity-level sandbox would raise parity but is a multi-day project.

Suggested follow-up: tighten macOS sandbox_init profile to include the specific syscall families that the Linux seccomp filter currently denies but the macOS profile does not; evaluate WinSandbox / AppContainer for Windows parity.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:52:54.756663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T07:52:54.756663Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b","title":"Testing Completeness Program: non-mock acceptance coverage and high-signal E2E evidence","description":"Background:
- The repo already has meaningful testing assets: `crates/fcp-e2e`, `crates/fcp-host/tests/host_connector_integration.rs`, `fcp-testkit`, connector `tests/integration.rs`, and structured JSONL reporting.
- That is still not the same thing as full pure-unit coverage without mocks/fake peers or complete end-to-end acceptance coverage with operator-grade logs.
- The current 2026-03-23 inventory says the workspace has 149 connector directories and 28 core crates; 139/149 connectors show unit-test signal, 100/149 have crate-local `tests/`, 84/149 have `fcp-e2e` coverage, 0/149 expose connector-level `no_mock_integration.rs`, only 8 connectors document verification bundles, and logging/replay evidence exists for 92/149 connectors.
- The user question behind this program is therefore settled: no, the repo does not yet have full unit coverage without fake-backed confidence, and no, it does not yet have complete non-mock E2E scripts with great detailed logging across the workspace.

Program goal:
- Make pure-unit coverage explicit and truthful, with fake-backed suites demoted to `deterministic_contract` rather than silently counting as final unit evidence.
- Give every core crate and connector family an archetype-appropriate acceptance path: `local_non_mock`, `host_e2e`, `live`, or a documented combination.
- Make every E2E-capable suite emit durable, redacted, replayable artifacts that let future agents debug failures without rediscovering the whole test setup.

Definition of done for the overall program:
- The coverage matrix and acceptance taxonomy stay current and are enforced.
- Shared harnesses exist for HTTP, streaming/webhook/session, and DB/storage/queue/browser/local-process boundaries.
- Connector-family sweeps close both pure-unit floor gaps and non-mock acceptance gaps.
- Nightly/live suites have an explicit operating model for secrets, cost, cleanup, and artifact retention.
- CI and maintainer playbooks make the whole testing story executable rather than aspirational.","notes":"Clarification: repo policy still forbids real external-service calls in unit tests. This program treats "no mocks/fake stuff" as a requirement for acceptance layers (`local_non_mock`, `host_e2e`, `live`) while `pure_unit` stays focused on local logic and truthful suite classification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:33.507188Z","created_by":"LilacLake","updated_at":"2026-03-25T00:34:42.461132Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T00:34:42.460968Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","quality","testing"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.1","title":"Audit current workspace test surface and publish a connector/core coverage gap matrix","description":"Background:
- Before changing test infrastructure, we need one authoritative inventory of what exists today.
- The current workspace mixes inline unit tests, `tests/integration.rs`, crate-level `no_mock_integration.rs`, `fcp-e2e` compliance suites, host subprocess tests, and many `wiremock`/`MockApiServer` suites.

Deliverables:
- Build a connector-by-connector and core-crate-by-core-crate matrix that records: unit tests present, integration tests present, `wiremock`/fake reliance, `no_mock_integration` presence, host-backed subprocess coverage, `fcp-e2e` coverage, logging/report schema validation, live/provider prerequisites, and known gaps.
- Explicitly classify which current tests are deterministic contract tests versus true non-mock acceptance tests.
- Group results by connector archetype so later waves can execute systematically instead of ad hoc.

Acceptance criteria:
- The matrix covers every current connector directory and every core crate that participates in runtime, policy, host, protocol, or observability behavior.
- Missing suites are prioritized by risk and user impact, not just by alphabetical order.
- The output is self-contained enough that future agents can plan from the matrix without repeating the archaeology pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseDesert","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:33.794376Z","created_by":"LilacLake","updated_at":"2026-03-23T08:31:23.144998Z","closed_at":"2026-03-23T08:31:23.144658Z","close_reason":"Published live test coverage and acceptance gap matrix in docs/testing/coverage-inventory.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coverage","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:33.794376Z","created_by":"LilacLake","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.10","title":"Developer-platform and automation connectors: non-mock acceptance wave","description":"Background:
- Developer-platform and automation connectors often look easy because they are HTTP APIs, but they also have rollout, artifact, webhook, and long-running job semantics that simple mocks miss.

Scope:
- GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, CircleCI, Package Registry, DockerHub, Make, n8n, Browser, MCP Bridge, Webhook Receiver, Vercel, Netlify, and related developer-platform or automation connectors.

Deliverables:
- Add non-mock acceptance coverage for auth, polling/job-state transitions, webhook or callback flows, artifact handling, pagination, and mutation safety semantics.
- Validate host-backed operation flows end to end, including logging and receipt/approval behavior where relevant.
- Close missing compliance E2E gaps in this family and demote mock-only suites to supplemental status.

Acceptance criteria:
- Developer-platform connectors are covered by acceptance suites that exercise real workflows, not just static HTTP payload fixtures.
- Failures produce enough detail to debug CI/automation regressions without manual repro.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:36.815187Z","created_by":"LilacLake","updated_at":"2026-03-24T06:03:22Z","closed_at":"2026-03-24T06:03:22Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["developer-platform","e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.10","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:36.815187Z","created_by":"LilacLake","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.10","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:43.323043Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.10","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:43.562472Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.10","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:43.807886Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.10","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:44.073569Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.10","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-49z0b.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-23T08:04:44.315094Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
